data.current_description:
Perform an action on an Instance.
Available actions are:
* `poweron`: Start a stopped Instance.
* `poweroff`: Fully stop the Instance and release the hypervisor slot.
* `stop_in_place`: Stop the Instance, but keep the slot on the hypervisor.
* `reboot`: Stop the instance and restart it.
* `backup`:  Create an image with all the volumes of an Instance.
* `terminate`: Delete the Instance along with its attached local volumes.
* `enable_routed_ip`: Migrate the Instance to the new network stack.

The `terminate` action will result in the deletion of `l_ssd` and `scratch` volumes types, `sbs_volume` volumes will only be detached.
If you want to preserve your `l_ssd` volumes, you should stop your Instance, detach the volumes to be preserved, then delete your Instance.

The `backup` action can be done with:
* No `volumes` key in the body: an image is created with snapshots of all the server volumes, except for the `scratch` volumes types.
* `volumes` key in the body with a dictionary as value, in this dictionary volumes UUID as keys and empty dictionaries as values : an image is created with the snapshots of the volumes in `volumes` key. `scratch` volumes types can't be shapshotted.
data.summary_suggestion: Perform an action on an Instance
data.description_suggestion: Perform an action on a specified Instance. Available actions: poweron (start stopped Instance), poweroff (stop and release hypervisor slot), stop_in_place (stop but retain slot), reboot (stop and restart), backup (create image from volumes), terminate (delete Instance and local volumes), enable_routed_ip (migrate to new network stack). The terminate action deletes l_ssd and scratch volumes but only detaches sbs_volume volumes. The backup action creates an image: without volumes parameter, snapshots all volumes except scratch; with volumes parameter (UUID keys with empty values), snapshots only specified volumes. Returns the action result or newly created Image object for backup actions.
data.reasoning: Summary 'Perform action' is too vague for AI agent routing. Description is comprehensive but lacks explicit return value information. Improved summary to be more specific, reorganized description for clarity, and added explicit return value details (action result or Image object for backup) to help AI agents understand outcomes.

data.current_description:
Perform an action on an Instance.
Available actions are:
* `poweron`: Start a stopped Instance.
* `poweroff`: Fully stop the Instance and release the hypervisor slot.
* `stop_in_place`: Stop the Instance, but keep the slot on the hypervisor.
* `reboot`: Stop the instance and restart it.
* `backup`:  Create an image with all the volumes of an Instance.
* `terminate`: Delete the Instance along with its attached local volumes.
* `enable_routed_ip`: Migrate the Instance to the new network stack.

The `terminate` action will result in the deletion of `l_ssd` and `scratch` volumes types, `sbs_volume` volumes will only be detached.
If you want to preserve your `l_ssd` volumes, you should stop your Instance, detach the volumes to be preserved, then delete your Instance.

The `backup` action can be done with:
* No `volumes` key in the body: an image is created with snapshots of all the server volumes, except for the `scratch` volumes types.
* `volumes` key in the body with a dictionary as value, in this dictionary volumes UUID as keys and empty dictionaries as values : an image is created with the snapshots of the volumes in `volumes` key. `scratch` volumes types can't be shapshotted.
data.summary_suggestion: Perform an action on an Instance
data.description_suggestion: Perform a specified action on an Instance. Available actions include: poweron (start stopped Instance), poweroff (stop and release hypervisor slot), stop_in_place (stop but retain slot), reboot (restart), backup (create image from volumes), terminate (delete Instance and local volumes), and enable_routed_ip (migrate to new network stack). The terminate action deletes l_ssd and scratch volumes but only detaches sbs_volume volumes. The backup action creates an image with snapshots of all volumes except scratch types; optionally specify a volumes dictionary with UUIDs as keys to snapshot only selected volumes. Returns the task status or created resource (e.g., Image for backup action).
data.reasoning: Summary 'Perform action' is too vague and generic. Description is comprehensive but lacks clarity on return values for different actions. Improved summary to be more specific and added information about what is returned to help AI agents understand the operation's effects and determine appropriate follow-up actions.
